items.headline: Strait of Hormuz Closes Again After US Refuses to Lift Naval Blockade
items.body: Iran shut the Strait of Hormuz on April 18 after briefly announcing it open, following the US refusal to end its naval blockade. A fifth of the world's oil passes through the strait, and several vessels were targeted after the closure was declared.

items.headline: IMF Warns Middle East War Is Pushing Global Economy Toward Recession
items.body: The IMF cut its 2026 global growth forecast and warned that a prolonged energy disruption could push growth to 2 percent, the threshold historically linked to global recession. Inflation could exceed 6 percent in the worst scenario.

items.headline: Mass Shooting in Wartime Kyiv Kills Six as Gunman Born in Russia Opens Fire
items.body: A gunman killed six people and wounded at least 14 outside a supermarket in central Kyiv on April 19 before police shot him dead. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy confirmed the attacker was born in Russia.

items.headline: Iran's Internet Blackout Reaches 50 Days With Door-to-Door Satellite Dish Seizures
items.body: Iran's nationwide internet shutdown passed 50 days, the longest ever recorded for a connected society, according to NetBlocks. Security forces began confiscating satellite dishes door to door after the government shut down Starlink access.

meanwhile.text: Researchers published results showing a malaria vaccine using modified RNA produced durable immunity in adults across three African trial sites.
meanwhile.search: mRNA malaria vaccine trial results published 2026

meanwhile.text: A Pew Research survey found that fewer than one in three adults under 30 in Western Europe now identify with any organised religion.
meanwhile.search: Pew Research young Europeans religion identity 2026

meanwhile.text: A large study published in The Lancet linked ultra-processed food consumption to measurably faster cognitive decline in adults over 50.
meanwhile.search: Lancet ultra-processed food cognitive decline study 2026

es.headline: Spain Bypasses Parliament to Legalise Up to 500,000 Undocumented Migrants by Royal Decree
es.body: Spain's government approved a royal decree on April 14 opening a residency and work permit process for undocumented migrants who can prove continuous residence before December 2025. Applications opened online April 16 and in person April 20, with the window closing June 30.

no.headline: Norway's Oil Exports Hit All-Time Record as Hormuz Closure Reshapes European Energy Supply
no.body: Norway exported crude oil worth NOK 57.4 billion in March, up 67.9% year on year, the highest monthly value ever recorded. European buyers are now routing demand toward Norwegian supply as Middle Eastern shipments remain blocked.

uk.headline: Royal Navy Shadowed Russian Frigate Escorting Sanctioned Tankers Through English Channel
uk.body: A Russian warship armed with anti-ship missiles escorted two sanctioned oil tankers through the Channel on April 9. Britain's new interception powers went untested as the Royal Navy sent only an auxiliary tanker in response.

uk.headline: UK Attorney General Rules Military Cannot Board Russian Shadow Fleet Vessels Under International Law
uk.body: Britain's top law officer determined that intercepting Russian vessels would likely breach international maritime law. The ruling undermines a policy the government announced weeks ago as a show of force against sanctions evasion.

nl.headline: Dutch Civil Servants Strike Forces Historic Parliamentary Shutdown and Restarts Wage Talks
nl.body: A nationwide strike by central government workers halted all debates in the Tweede Kamer for the first time ever. Negotiations resumed April 18 after unions won a commitment to abandon the zero-wage-increase policy for 2026.
